findings.description: The knowledge file has `ValidationContext<CustomerEntity> context` (with correct closing `>`), but the source has the unclosed generic `ValidationContext<CustomerEntity context` — a documentation typo. The knowledge file silently corrects the source error, adding a `>` that is not in the source.
findings.source_evidence: Source line: `public static void validate(ValidationContext<CustomerEntity context) throws Exception {`

findings.description: The knowledge file adds an alt text description 'ラジオボタンで精査条件を切り替える入力画面' for the screen image. The source RST image directive has no alt text (`.. image:: ../_image/examples/containsKeyView.png` with only a `:scale:` option), so this text is invented by the knowledge file.
findings.source_evidence: Source: `.. image:: ../_image/examples/containsKeyView.png\n   :scale: 55` — no alt text present

findings.description: `@ValidateFor` and `ValidationContext` both appear in the `CustomerEntity.validate()` code example in s3 but are absent from s3's hints. They are meaningful API names a user might ask about in the context of code-value validation.
findings.source_evidence: Source s3 code: `@ValidateFor("validate")` and `ValidationContext<CustomerEntity context)` used in CustomerEntity class
